top5Opportunities.statement: Verify blank compliance with headspace stack-up before machining
Detect dimensional drift before full lot committed to machining
No traceability gaps in defense prime audits
Qualify new custom die in under 8 weeks
Tooling lead not delaying program by >4 weeks

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of profile cross-section dimensions deviating more than +-0.05mm from specification
needs.importanceRationale: Receiver pocket fit rejection at CNC setup

needs.statement: Minimize variability in receiver profile cross-section dimensions across bars within batch exceeding +-0.03mm
needs.importanceRationale: Batch consistency critical for CNC fixture setup

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood that barrel blank profiles deviate from roundness 0.01mm TIR
needs.importanceRationale: Bore concentricity depends on blank TIR at gun-drilling

needs.statement: Increase ability to specify custom asymmetric cross-section profiles for pistol slide rail within +-0.02mm
needs.importanceRationale: IT8 tolerance critical for slide-frame fit

needs.statement: Minimize time to qualify new custom profile die from drawing to first-article acceptance in under 8 weeks
needs.importanceRationale: 6-12 week tooling lead is dominant P1 burden

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood that custom tooling lead time delays firearms program launch by >4 weeks
needs.importanceRationale: Program-critical path impact from die lead time

needs.statement: Minimize time to source and qualify alternative steel profile blank supplier
needs.importanceRationale: Single-source risk on custom die geometries

needs.statement: Increase ability to verify incoming blanks comply with headspace tolerance stack-up before machining
needs.importanceRationale: Headspace compliance is safety-critical; must be verified before machining

needs.statement: Minimize time to detect systematic dimensional drift before full delivery lot committed to machining
needs.importanceRationale: Drift causes cascade of scrap across full batch

needs.statement: Increase ability to recover within 2 weeks from tooling failure disrupting profile blank supply
needs.importanceRationale: Military receiver program cannot tolerate >2 week gap

needs.statement: Minimize likelihood of EN 10204 3.1 material test reports missing or incomplete at inspection
needs.importanceRationale: Safety-critical; cert completeness gates machining release

needs.statement: Minimize volume of machined material removed from incoming profile blanks to reach final receiver geometry
needs.importanceRationale: Near-net-shape eliminates 40-60% machining; bar stock wastes 60-70%

needs.statement: Minimize traceability document gaps in steel profile delivery records during defense prime audits
needs.importanceRationale: Defense prime auditor and proof house inspector must see clean traceability chain
